original_rubric_note: Produced before monorepo migration, when the rubric lived at E:/AI/cognitive-load-audit/cognitive-load-audit.md.

evidence_mode.limitations: Live access to outlook.live.com blocked by auth gate (no cached session). Findings draw on Microsoft canonical Support documentation + side-by-side comparison image + community Q&A. Strong for vendor-described intent and command-path mappings. Weak for felt cost, keyboard-shortcut behavior, edge-case interactions, and verification of community-described claims. Does NOT replace live observation of Outlook under real task state.

target.value: Microsoft Outlook — Classic Ribbon vs Simplified Ribbon (desktop + new Outlook)
primary_sources: Microsoft's official Classic-vs-Simplified comparison image embedded in the first article
Microsoft documentation of recall-message paths in both ribbon modes
Microsoft documentation of add-ins / More apps / pinning
Microsoft Q&A community responses on new Outlook customization
surfaces_audited: Outlook desktop ribbon tab row (Classic vs Simplified comparison image)
Home tab content mapping per Microsoft reference guide (Inbox / Message / Calendar / Contact / Task categories)
Three-dots overflow documented contents (Rules / Respond with Meeting / Read Aloud)
Ribbon customization affordances per Microsoft documentation and Q&A community

context.user_type: Outlook user, full-fluency in Classic Ribbon, considering Simplified Ribbon
context.task: audit Simplified Ribbon's preservation of Classic Ribbon power; calibrate Section 5 taxonomy via documentation
context.dense_state: implied by Microsoft's documentation scope; not directly verified
context.low_bandwidth_state: auditor not in a constrained state
ai_features_present: Copilot in modern Outlook, not in scope
context.method: Documentation fallback. Auth gate prevented live audit; pivoted to vendor-canonical Microsoft Support docs per v0.2 Section 7 responsive-evidence caveat.
context.viewport_render_limitation: n/a — not a viewport issue. The limitation is the absence of a live signed-in session entirely.

text_load.notes: Microsoft documentation is plain-language. No Section 0 findings.

hidden_load.notes: Three findings touch Section 2 as secondary. Section 5 is the primary classification for each.

state_shift.notes: CL-18 medium — unlabeled caret toggle for Classic <-> Simplified switch. Below Fail threshold.

power_preservation.notes: Four Highs (CL-15 Folder-tab Hidden, CL-16 Tell-Me-only Hidden, CL-17 ...-overflow Hidden, CL-19 reduced-shape customization Hidden) + one Medium (CL-20 reference-guide meta-evidence). Three Highs trigger Section Fail. **No Critical, no Removed observations.** Section 5 fails on hidden-load accumulation, not on power removal. Earlier draft of PT2 produced CL-15 and CL-19 as Removed findings; honest reading of Microsoft's docs reclassifies both to Hidden — capability preserved via alternate paths.

configuration_cost.notes: CL-19 secondary section (Configuration Cost) reflects the reduced-shape customization. CL-18 toggle caret labeling. Below Fail threshold.

evidence.notes: All findings rest on Microsoft documentation (Observed for direct Microsoft Support articles) plus community Q&A (Inferred). Live signed-in verification would promote CL-19 and several others to Observed.

findings.title: Folder tab is absent from Simplified Ribbon tab row
findings.evidence_summary: Microsoft's side-by-side comparison image shows Classic with seven tabs (incl. Folder), Simplified with six (no Folder). Folder commands reachable via right-click in nav pane, `...` overflow, or Tell Me search. Reclassified from Removed-with-workaround (earlier overclaim) to Hidden — capability preserved through alternate entry points; only the tab-row visual entry point is removed.

findings.title: Hidden commands reachable only via 'Tell me what you want to do' search
findings.evidence_summary: Microsoft docs explicitly direct users to Tell Me as the first-line solution for missing buttons.

findings.title: `...` overflow contains accessibility features (Read Aloud) and power features (Rules)
findings.evidence_summary: Microsoft docs name Rules, Respond with Meeting, Read Aloud as `...`-overflow contents. Hiding accessibility features behind discovery-cost is recursive. Pin/unpin can restore them — Hidden-with-restoration.

findings.title: Switch-back to Classic Ribbon is an unlabeled caret in lower-right corner
findings.evidence_summary: Microsoft docs describe the caret position; absence of label description implies unlabeled.

findings.title: Granular ribbon customization is reduced (not removed) in new Outlook + Simplified
findings.surface: ribbon customization affordances in new Outlook
findings.evidence_summary: Microsoft docs confirm 'users can customize commands shown in the simplified ribbon' (pin/unpin available). Community Q&A describes granular custom-tab / custom-group authoring (Classic Customize Ribbon dialog) as more limited or absent. Reclassified from Removed-without-equivalent / Critical (earlier overclaim) to Hidden / reduced-shape Medium — basic customization concept preserved via pin/unpin; granular authoring path is reduced.

findings.title: Vendor 'Simplified Ribbon reference' guide is meta-evidence of Hidden classification
findings.evidence_summary: Direct: `simplified-ribbon-reference-792ddf33-...` exists with five command categories mapping Classic to Simplified. Vendor publishing a compensation doc is structural evidence of Hidden severity.

open_questions.summary: CL-19 reclassification (Hidden / reduced-shape Medium) rests on community Q&A; not directly verified via signed-in new Outlook.
open_questions.resolution_path: Sign into new Outlook, enable Simplified Ribbon, attempt to create a custom tab or group. Refine description of what's preserved vs reduced. Promote to Observed.

open_questions.summary: Live Outlook surface not exercised at all. Per-button cost, keyboard-shortcut behavior, edge-case interactions not directly tested.
open_questions.resolution_path: Re-run with signed-in Outlook session. Capture screenshots of actual Simplified Ribbon. Exercise toggle, Tell Me, `...` overflow, pin/unpin, customization affordances.

exit_criteria_check.every_removed_claim_names_feature_and_ac: n/a — no Removed claims survived honest reclassification
exit_criteria_check.scorecard_confirms_removed_severity_defa: deferred — PT2-doc-fallback did not produce live-observed Removed cases. v0.3 sub-distinction recommendation is parked pending a future PT on a target with true feature subsetting.

rubric_v0_3_recommendations: DEFERRED: the proposed Removed sub-distinction (removed_with_workaround vs removed_without_equivalent) is parked. PT2-doc-fallback did not produce live-observed Removed cases — the original draft overclaimed two findings as Removed, both reclassified to Hidden on honest reading.

Document the 'vendor reference guide as Hidden meta-evidence' pattern (CL-20). When a vendor publishes a separate documentation artifact whose purpose is to help users find moved features, that itself is structural evidence of Hidden classification severity. Worth folding into Section 5's failure-modes list.

Future PT3 should target a product with strict feature subsetting (a lite/free tier with documented absent features, a feature-stripped mobile app, an enterprise-vs-consumer split). Outlook Simplified Ribbon was the wrong target for Removed calibration — it is mostly a Hidden/Delayed power model, not a Removed one.
Continue carrying OQ-01 forward across all PTs.
